families.label: Action
families.description: Tagged glossary entries that define standard combat and gameplay actions.

families.label: Area of Effect
families.description: Tagged glossary entries that define shapes such as cones and cubes.

families.label: Attitude
families.description: Tagged glossary entries describing NPC disposition and social stance.

families.label: Condition
families.description: Temporary game states with defined effects and endings.

families.label: Hazard
families.description: Environmental and situational dangers used during exploration and play.

entries.label: Ability Check
entries.summary: A D20 Test representing the use of one of the six abilities, or a skill associated with one, to overcome a challenge.

entries.label: Action
entries.summary: On your turn, you can take one action, chosen from the standard list or from special actions granted by features.

entries.label: Advantage
entries.summary: Roll two d20s and use the higher roll.

entries.label: Disadvantage
entries.summary: Roll two d20s and use the lower roll.

entries.label: Bonus Action
entries.summary: An additional action on your turn that you can take only when a feature, spell, or rule specifically grants one.

entries.label: Reaction
entries.summary: An instant response to a trigger. After taking a Reaction, you cannot take another until the start of your next turn.

entries.label: Concentration
entries.summary: Some spells and effects require Concentration to remain active. If Concentration ends, the effect ends.

entries.label: Condition
entries.summary: A temporary game state. Conditions do not stack with themselves unless a rule says otherwise. Exhaustion is an exception.

entries.label: Cover
entries.summary: Cover protects a target behind it. The strongest available degree of cover applies.

entries.label: Difficult Terrain
entries.summary: Difficult Terrain increases the movement cost to enter an affected space.

entries.label: Climbing
entries.summary: While climbing, each foot of movement costs 1 extra foot, or 2 extra feet in Difficult Terrain.

entries.label: Climb Speed
entries.summary: A Climb Speed lets you traverse vertical surfaces without paying the extra movement cost normally associated with climbing.

entries.label: Crawling
entries.summary: While crawling, each foot of movement costs 1 extra foot, or 2 extra feet in Difficult Terrain.

entries.label: Speed
entries.summary: A creature’s Speed determines how far it can move on its turn.

entries.label: Opportunity Attack
entries.summary: A common Reaction triggered by specific enemy movement in combat.

entries.label: Short Rest
entries.summary: A period of downtime used by many class features, recovery mechanics, and pacing systems.

entries.label: Long Rest
entries.summary: A major rest period used for full or substantial recovery, daily refreshes, and many campaign effects.

entries.label: Temporary Hit Points
entries.summary: A buffer of extra Hit Points that absorbs damage before normal Hit Points are lost.

entries.label: Resistance
entries.summary: Resistance reduces damage of a specified type.

entries.label: Vulnerability
entries.summary: Vulnerability increases damage of a specified type.

entries.label: Immunity
entries.summary: Immunity prevents the specified damage or effect from affecting the target.

entries.label: Blindsight
entries.summary: A special sense defined in the rules glossary that allows perception without relying on normal sight.

entries.label: Darkvision
entries.summary: A special sense defined in the rules glossary that improves perception in darkness.

entries.label: Tremorsense
entries.summary: A special sense defined in the rules glossary that detects through vibrations or ground contact.

entries.label: Truesight
entries.summary: A special sense defined in the rules glossary that pierces forms of visual deception or obstruction.

entries.label: Lightly Obscured
entries.summary: A lightly obscured area imposes Disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.

entries.label: Heavily Obscured
entries.summary: A heavily obscured area is opaque. Creatures are effectively Blinded when trying to see into it.

entries.label: Travel Pace
entries.summary: Outside combat, a group can travel at Fast, Normal, or Slow pace, each with distance and skill effects.

entries.label: Blinded
entries.summary: You cannot see and suffer the usual combat and check penalties of blindness.

entries.label: Charmed
entries.summary: You cannot attack the charmer or target them with damaging abilities or magical effects, and the charmer has Advantage on social checks involving you.

entries.label: Deafened
entries.summary: You cannot hear and suffer the normal consequences of that loss of perception.

entries.label: Exhaustion
entries.summary: A special condition that can stack and worsen over time.

entries.label: Frightened
entries.summary: A fear state that impairs action and positioning according to the condition’s rules.

entries.label: Grappled
entries.summary: A creature held in place or physically restricted by another creature or effect.

entries.label: Incapacitated
entries.summary: A creature that cannot take actions, bonus actions, or reactions.

entries.label: Invisible
entries.summary: A creature cannot be seen normally and gains the usual offensive and defensive implications of invisibility.

entries.label: Paralyzed
entries.summary: A severe control state that prevents meaningful action or movement.

entries.label: Petrified
entries.summary: A creature is transformed into inert matter and suffers the normal consequences of petrification.

entries.label: Poisoned
entries.summary: A creature suffers impaired effectiveness due to poison.

entries.label: Prone
entries.summary: A creature is on the ground and subject to the normal movement and attack implications of being prone.

entries.label: Restrained
entries.summary: A creature is bound or held in place and suffers the usual penalties to movement and combat.

entries.label: Stunned
entries.summary: A severe disruption state that leaves a creature unable to act effectively.

entries.label: Unconscious
entries.summary: A creature is unaware and helpless, with the normal combat consequences of unconsciousness.

entries.label: Cone
entries.summary: A Cone extends in straight lines from a point of origin in a chosen direction, and its width equals its distance from the origin at any point.

entries.label: Creature
entries.summary: Any being in the game, including a player character, is a creature.

entries.label: Creature Type
entries.summary: Every creature has a rules tag identifying its type.
